'''Threfal''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Endor" /> located in the [[Threfal system/Legends|Threfal system]], a part of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Iska sector/Legends|Iska sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Threfal had a [[Atmosphere/Legends|Type I]] breathable atmosphere and was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Plant/Legends|plant]] known as the [[Meatvine/Legends|meatvine]], which could be used as [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]].<ref name="Endor" />

==Behind the scenes==
Threfal was mentioned in the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," which was authored by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Daniel Wallace]], and [[Jason Fry]], and was published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Endor" /> on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Threfal [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world Threfal, in grid square J-16.<ref name="TEA" />
